content.full_text:
December 16, 1986


Thomas J. Harron
City Attorney
City of Chula Vista
276 Fourth Avenue
Chula Vista, CA 92010

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-86-316

Dear Mr. Harron:

You have requested advice concerning Chula Vista City Councilmember David Malcolm's duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Since you have not provided us with all of the material facts relevant to any particular decision, we consider your letter to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).

You have asked for advice regarding whether Councilmember Malcolm may participate in decisions which will affect a general partner in a business in which Councilmember Malcolm is a limited partner. You have indicated that Councilmember Malcolm is involved in several different limited partnerships.

This issue was thoroughly dealt with in the Commission's Nord Opinion, 8 FPPC Opinions 6 (No. 83-004, Oct. 4, 1983) (copy enclosed). The opinion should be reviewed carefully in order to determine Mr. Malcolm's obligations. It should be noted that since the time Nord was issued, the

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Thomas J. Harron
December 16, 1986
Page 2


Commission has adopted Regulation 18236 (copy enclosed) which
defines the term "otherwise related business entity."

If you should have any questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel


John G. McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JGM:plh
Enclosures

CITY OF
CHULA VISTA
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

November 14, 1986


John McLean
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Mr. McLean:

As a follow-up to our telephone conversation on Thursday,
November 13, 1986, I would like reinstate my request for a letter
opinion which I first made in a letter to you dated July 18,
1986. It involves a fact situation in which Councilman David
Malcolm is a limited partner in property south of the City of
Chula Vista in the Otay Mesa area. The general partner owns
property within the City of Chula Vista which is unrelated to the
other property and which he wishes to develop. A number of items
will be coming before the Chula Vista City Council on this second
property which is owned by Malcolm's partner. May Councilman
Malcolm participate in votes on these unrelated projects?

If you need further information, you may contact Councilman
Malcolm directly. His phone at work is 619-425-7080. If I can
be of any further assistance, please feel free to contact me.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.

Very truly yours,

Thomas J. Harron
City Attorney

2200a


276 FOURTH AVENUE/CHULA VISTA, CALIFORNIA 92010/(619) 691-5037

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 13, 1986




Thomas J. Harron
City Attorney
City of Chula Vista
276 Fourth Avenue
Chula Vista, CA  92010

                                          Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                               Our File No. A-86-241

Dear Mr. Harron:

     This is to confirm that you have withdrawn your request for
advice on behalf of Councilman David Malcolm.  If in the future
you wish advice on this matter please let me know.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          [signature]
                                          John G. McLean
                                          Counsel
                                          Legal Division

JGM:plh




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 25, 1986




Thomas J. Harron
Chula Vista City Attorney
276 Fourth Avenue
Chula Vista, CA  92010

Re:  86-241

Dear Mr. Harron:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received on July 22, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

John G. McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JGM:plh




428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

CITY OF
CHULA VISTA
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

FPPC
JUL 22 8 19 AM '86

July 18, 1986


John McLean
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Mr. McLean:

This letter is a follow-up to our conversation on Wednesday,
July 16, 1986 with regard to a question that Councilman David
Malcolm brought up on conflict of interest.  It involves the
following fact situation:

     Councilman Malcolm and a partner have made an offer to
     purchase a piece of property.  The purchase amount is
     presently established.  The purchase is contingent upon
     the property receiving necessary approvals from
     appropriate governmental agencies (including the
     City of Chula Vista).  It is estimated that it will
     take at least two years to get the approvals necessary
     to develop the property.  Councilman Malcolm and his
     partner are equal partners.  May Councilman Malcolm
     vote on projects proposed by his partner unrelated to
     this project?

In our phone conversation, you advised me that the Commission
discussed this issue in November and changed its position from
earlier opinions, so that now Councilman Malcolm may vote on
unrelated projects.  When I informed Councilman Malcolm of this
advice, he asked me to request an advice letter from you.  It is
my understanding that you have not prepared any advice letters
since the November meeting, and that this one would be the first
one to address this subject.

We appreciate your assistance in this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Thomas J. Harron
                                        City Attorney

TJH:clb
1792a

276 FOURTH AVENUE/CHULA VISTA, CALIFORNIA 92010/(619) 691-5037

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 20, 1986


Thomas J. Harron
Chula Vista City Attorney
276 Fourth Avenue
Chula Vista, CA 92010

Re: 86-316

Dear Mr. Harron:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on November 17, 1986, by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. Commission Regulation 18329(b)(2) (copy
enclosed) requires a request for formal written advice to
include a specific statement that you have been authorized to
request the advice by the person whose duties under the Act are
in question. Please send us a supplemental letter stating that
you are so authorized by the official on whose behalf you have
requested advice. Please also include in your letter the
mailing address of the official.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days after we receive your supplemental
letter. You also should be aware that your letter and our
response are public records which may be disclosed to the
public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. If you
have any questions about your advice request, you may contact
John G. McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at
(916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh
cc: David Malcolm

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Patsy
pls. prepare
a yellow
approval
sheet for
this packet
leave this
pg or top
Thanks
D.

Diane—
After receiving Harron
advice request, I called to
find out more specifics
about the limited pathways
refund to in his letter. He
did not know the answer
but said Malcolm was
involved in several
partnerships and needs
general advice applicable

to all of them. I told
Harron that the answers
were all contained in the
Nerd opinion. He asked
if we would put that in
writing.
Although we could decline
to give advice because
they ain't requesting it for
on a specific decision), I

think it's a good idea to
at least give the attached
informal advice. Considering
Malcolm is apparently on
the shady side and this
would document that he
has been given advice
on these issues.
I have intentionally not
summarized Nerd in the
letter because I want them
to read the opinion. - JFM

sections.question_synthetic: May Councilmember David Malcolm participate in City Council decisions affecting a general partner in a business in which Malcolm is a limited partner, and may he vote on unrelated projects proposed by his partner?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC referenced its prior Nord Opinion (83-004) and newly adopted Regulation 18236 defining "otherwise related business entity" as the applicable framework for determining Malcolm's conflict of interest obligations regarding limited partnership interests and related business decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a compilation of multiple related letters spanning July-December 1986. The primary FPPC response (December 16, 1986) provides synthetic conclusion but no formal Q/A sections. Initial request was withdrawn (August 13) then reinstated (November 14). Some source documents have OCR quality issues affecting readability.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May Councilmember David Malcolm participate in City Council decisions affecting a general partner in a business in which Malcolm is a limited partner, and may he vote on unrelated projects proposed by his partner?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC referenced its prior Nord Opinion (83-004) and newly adopted Regulation 18236 defining "otherwise related business entity" as the applicable framework for determining Malcolm's conflict of interest obligations regarding limited partnership interests and related business decisions.

embedding.summary: This file contains multiple 1986 FPPC documents regarding Councilmember David Malcolm's conflict of interest duties related to his limited partnership interests. The correspondence includes an initial request, withdrawal, reinstatement request, and final FPPC advice letter referencing the Nord Opinion and Regulation 18236.
